body.u: I can remember well when I left school. My last day at school was a Friday and I had four last day at home before I left to go into the Navy. I first went down to Plymouth and spent six weeks at Torport CaditCadet training centre. During that time I received my results of my ‘o’ levels that I had taken the month before. I passed all of them except my art exam. I then signed up with the Navy for 12 years. I think that was the biggest desciondecision I had ever made in my whole life. And I settled down to spend a year a Naval collagecollege.

I can remember well the first motorbike I had, I brought it new on the HP on my 17th birthday. I had a great time with that old bike. I have still got it out in the shed. But as we haven’t been {illegible} to pay petrol for the last thirty years it has just been rotting away. I spent a year at Navalnavalcollagecollege and then I went to sea for a year. I think that was one of the best years of my whole life. At first I was on HMS Neptune a First class destroyer. There no such thing as destroyer now not since the world council abolished all earth based navysnavies. On that destroyer I went to Hong Kong. Fantastic place is HongKongHong Kong then we moved to Australia. I remember Sidney well they was trying to break the world record distance for man powered flight. Our Capt. gave us permission to form a team and have an attempt. I think our machine only ever got of the ground for a couple of seconds. And only moved about ten yards.
After that I came back to Britain and was stationed at Scotland I think that is World council sector 90 today.

I met the wife there. I remember her as a really good looking girl who wouldn’t touchetouch me with a bargepole. whatsWhat's a pole. Well it was a wooden stick about 15 metres long. But one day some bloke had dropped her and I went over to try my lucky. We have never been apart since. We were married a year later. Because I was married it put an end to my traverlingtraveling by ship. But I was posted to HongKongHong Kong after my training. And we lived happerlyhappily there for several years. I had had about 2 promotions at the time and I was offered a commission. To be chief engineer of Rosath in Sector 90. Well as the situation was getting a bit dodgey with the Chinese me and the wife decided that move would be the best thing.

And then your Dad came along your dad was a big baby. When your father was about 5 or 6 six I had to decide wheatherwhether to stay in the Navy for another 15 years or wheatherwhether I was going to going out into civy street.

At about that time the world council was coming into being and the 2nd Jesus cult was reaching its peak. I thought it was best if I looked for a job in civy street and as I had worked with some of the best marine propulsion equipment going at the time a applied for a job as chief engineer for a fleet of Mechantmerchant submarines. The pay was triple the amount I was receiveingreceiving and my family was growing very fast I took the job and here I am sitting on the board of director and money in the world bank.
